tools.name: BRICKSTORM
tools.evidence_snippet: BRICKSTORM is a custom Executable and Linkable Format (ELF) Go- or Rust-based backdoor (eight originally analyzed samples are Go-based, and two of the three new samples in the Dec. 19, 2025, update are Rust-based).

summary: BRICKSTORM is a custom Executable and Linkable Format (ELF) Go- or Rust-based backdoor used by PRC state-sponsored cyber actors for long-term persistence on victim systems.

timeline.event: Initial access to web server through web shell [T1505.003]
timeline.evidence_snippet: At the victim organization where CISA conducted an incident response engagement, PRC state-sponsored cyber actors accessed a web server on April 11, 2024.

timeline.event: Lateral movement from web server to domain controller using RDP and credentials associated with a second service account [T1078]
timeline.evidence_snippet: On April 12, 2024, the cyber actors moved laterally from the web server to a domain controller within the internal network using RDP and credentials associated with a second service account.
